title: Consumers Report Significant Improvements in Credit Market Experiences and Expectations

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York released results from its June 2015 SCE Credit Access Survey showing a significant improvement in credit market experiences, with the share of successful credit applicants reaching a new high and rejection rates falling to series lows since October 2013. Application rates increased from 46.1% to 47.5%, while rejection rates per applicant dropped from 20.0% to 13.8%. The improvement was most pronounced for borrowers with credit scores under 680.
key_points: 40.9% of respondents applied for credit and were granted, 6.6% applied and were rejected, 6.6% were discouraged from applying despite needing credit
Rejection rates per applicant fell from 20.0% to 13.8% in February 2015 comparison, reaching new series lows
Rejection rates for lowest creditworthy group dropped to 33.4% from 52.6% in February 2015
Rejection rates declined for all age groups; those aged 41-59 saw a 7.7 percentage point drop, those 60+ saw a 9.9 percentage point drop
Expected application likelihood over next 12 months increased for credit card limit, mortgage and auto loan applications to new series highs
Proportion of respondents likely to apply for at least one credit type remained steady at 29%
why_it_matters: This survey provides insight into household credit access conditions, which can indicate broader financial market health and consumer borrowing capacity. Declining rejection rates suggest easing credit conditions and improved lender appetite for risk.
macro_context: The survey results indicate easing credit conditions for consumers in mid-2015, with improved approval rates across all demographics. This suggests strengthening household access to credit, which could support consumer spending and broader economic activity.
important_numbers: 40.9% - share of respondents who applied and were granted credit
6.6% - share who applied and were rejected
33.4% - rejection rate for lowest credit score group (down from 52.6%)
14.2% - share reporting voluntary account closing or credit limit reduction
29% - proportion likely to apply for at least one credit type over next 12 months

issues_found: Published July 14, 2015 - approximately 10.5 years before TODAY; severely stale

Topic mismatch: SOURCE METADATA lists target as 'market structure' but content is about consumer credit access survey, not market structure in the sense of trading venues, market making, clearing, or settlement operations
Historical survey data with no direct relevance to current market structure or macro catalysts for 2026
Low value for RAG purposes given age and topic mismatch

human_review.notes: Source is stale (2015) and does not align with market structure topic in the traditional sense of market operations and plumbing. Recommend rejection or re-categorization.

notes: This source is not relevant to market structure (in the sense of trading venues, market operations, clearing, settlement, or market microstructure) or macro catalysts.
